questions.question: What makes sound?
questions.questionThai: อะไรสร้างเสียง?
questions.explanation: Sound is made when something vibrates (moves back and forth quickly).

questions.question: How does sound travel?
questions.questionThai: เสียงเดินทางอย่างไร?
questions.explanation: Sound travels in waves through air and other materials.

questions.question: What is volume?
questions.questionThai: ความดังคืออะไร?
options: How loud or soft a sound is
questions.explanation: Volume is how loud or soft a sound is.

questions.question: What is pitch?
questions.questionThai: ระดับเสียงคืออะไร?
options: How high or low a sound is
questions.explanation: Pitch is how high or low a sound is.

questions.question: Which has a higher pitch?
questions.questionThai: อันไหนมีระดับเสียงสูงกว่า?
questions.explanation: A bird chirping has a higher pitch than a lion's roar, thunder, or drum beats.

questions.question: What is an echo?
questions.questionThai: เสียงสะท้อนคืออะไร?
options: Sound bouncing back from a surface
questions.explanation: An echo is sound that bounces back to you from a hard surface.

questions.question: What part of your ear vibrates when sound waves hit it?
questions.questionThai: ส่วนใดของหูคุณสั่นเมื่อคลื่นเสียงกระทบมัน?
questions.explanation: The eardrum is a thin piece of skin that vibrates when sound waves hit it.

questions.question: Which material does sound travel through FASTEST?
questions.questionThai: วัสดุใดที่เสียงเดินทางผ่านได้เร็วที่สุด?
questions.explanation: Sound travels fastest through solids like metal because particles are packed tightly together.

questions.question: Why can't sound travel through outer space?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมเสียงไม่สามารถเดินทางผ่านอวกาศได้?
options: There are no particles to vibrate
questions.explanation: Sound needs particles to travel. Outer space is a vacuum with no particles, so sound cannot travel there.

questions.question: What makes a sound louder?
questions.questionThai: อะไรทำให้เสียงดังขึ้น?
questions.explanation: Louder sounds come from bigger vibrations (higher amplitude), which have more energy.

questions.question: What makes a sound higher in pitch?
questions.questionThai: อะไรทำให้เสียงมีระดับสูงขึ้น?
questions.explanation: Higher pitch sounds come from faster vibrations (higher frequency).

questions.question: In the reading passage, why did the longer string make a lower sound?
questions.questionThai: ในบทอ่าน ทำไมสายที่ยาวกว่าถึงสร้างเสียงต่ำกว่า?
questions.explanation: Longer strings vibrate more slowly, creating a lower frequency and lower pitch sound.

questions.question: Why do soft materials like carpet make rooms quieter?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมวัสดุนุ่มเช่นพรมทำให้ห้องเงียบกว่า?
questions.explanation: Soft materials absorb sound waves instead of reflecting them, making spaces quieter.

questions.question: In the reading passage, why did the khim sound different in the large music hall?
questions.questionThai: ในบทอ่าน ทำไมขิมถึงมีเสียงต่างกันในห้องดนตรีใหญ่?
options: Hard walls reflected sound and created echoes
questions.explanation: The hard walls of the music hall reflected sound waves, making the music richer and creating echoes.

questions.question: Why can you feel vibrations when you touch a speaker playing music?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมคุณสามารถรู้สึกการสั่นเมื่อสัมผัสลำโพงที่กำลังเล่นเพลง?
options: The speaker vibrates to create sound waves
questions.explanation: Speakers vibrate back and forth to push air and create sound waves. You can feel these vibrations.

questions.question: How do animals like bats use echoes?
questions.questionThai: สัตว์เช่นค้างคาวใช้เสียงสะท้อนอย่างไร?
questions.explanation: Bats use echolocation - they make sounds and listen for echoes bouncing off objects to find food and navigate.

questions.question: Why can very loud sounds damage your hearing?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมเสียงที่ดังมากถึงทำลายการได้ยินของคุณได้?
options: High-energy vibrations can damage the delicate parts of your ear
questions.explanation: Very loud sounds have high energy vibrations that can damage the sensitive eardrum and tiny hairs in your inner ear.

questions.question: Why does a thick guitar string make a lower sound than a thin string?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมสายกีตาร์หนาถึงสร้างเสียงต่ำกว่าสายบาง?
options: Thick strings vibrate more slowly than thin strings
questions.explanation: Thick strings have more mass and vibrate more slowly, creating a lower frequency and lower pitch.

questions.question: Why can you sometimes hear sounds through walls?
questions.questionThai: ทำไมบางครั้งคุณสามารถได้ยินเสียงผ่านกำแพง?
options: Sound can travel through solid materials
questions.explanation: Sound can travel through solid materials like walls because solids have particles that can vibrate and pass the sound along.

questions.question: What happens to sound energy as it travels farther from its source?
questions.questionThai: เกิดอะไรขึ้นกับพลังงานเสียงเมื่อมันเดินทางไกลจากแหล่งกำเนิด?
options: It spreads out and becomes weaker
questions.explanation: As sound waves travel away from their source, the energy spreads out over a larger area, making the sound weaker and quieter.
